perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall begin on the date hereof (the "Effective Date") and shall continue for a period of 12 months thereafter (the "Period") in full force and effect until it is terminated in accordance with this Section 3.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall begin on the date hereof (the "Effective Date") and shall continue indefinitely unless terminated earlier as provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the defined term to 'continue indefinitely' unless terminated, it contradicts section 3(b) which speaks of an additional period of 1 year. The contradiction lies in that it is an indefinite period and therefore there would be no need to specify an additional one-year period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Diplomat or Tadeo, if such party is not in default of the terms of this Agreement, may extend the term of this Agreement for an additional one year ("Additional Period"), provided the extending party gives the other party at least sixty (60) days advance written notice before the end of the Period. If either party elects to extend the Agreement for the Additional Period, all other terms and conditions of this Agreement shall continue during the Additional Period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the contract term from a fixed 12-month period to an indefinite period. This directly contradicts section (b), which provides a mechanism for extending the term for an "Additional Period" of one year. If the term is already indefinite, the provision for a one-year extension becomes logically inconsistent and unnecessary, creating a clear contradiction regarding the intended duration of the agreement and the applicability of the extension clause. The original text specifies a finite "Period" that can be extended, while the changed text eliminates this finite period, thereby directly undermining the contradicted text (b).

perturbation.original_text: Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate equal to fifteen (15%) percent per annum.
perturbation.changed_text: Late payments may accrue interest at a rate to be determined by Tadeo, not exceeding twenty (20%) percent per annum.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a fixed interest rate of 15% per annum for late payments. The modified text introduces discretion, stating that late payments 'may' accrue interest and allows Tadeo to determine the rate, up to a maximum of 20%. This contradicts the original fixed rate, creating uncertainty regarding the applicable interest rate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Diplomat will pay to Tadeo when due a fee for each of the Services equal to the amount described in EXHIBIT A hereto relating to each such Service; PROVIDED, that in the event Diplomat terminates this Agreement in accordance with Section 3 hereof, the fee for the provision of each - 2 - terminated Service shall cease to accrue on and after the effective date of such termination. In the event that Diplomat terminates this Agreement other than in accordance with Section 3, Diplomat shall be obligated to pay for the Services in accordance with the fee schedule contained on EXHIBIT A throughout the balance of the Period (as hereinafter defined) as though Tadeo continued to provide the terminated Services through the balance of the Period. Late payments may accrue interest at a rate to be determined by Tadeo, not exceeding twenty (20%) percent per annum.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a fixed interest rate. The changed text introduces optionality ('may') and gives Tadeo discretion to determine the interest rate (up to 20%), which directly contradicts the notion of a fixed rate established earlier. The contradicted text contains the changed text, meaning the interest rate is now subject to Tadeo's discretion, conflicting with the initial agreement of a fixed 15% rate.

perturbation.original_text: Diplomat may, at its own expense, examine and copy those books and records as provided in this Section 4. Such books, records and accounts will be maintained in a manner that allows Diplomat to separate these matters from those relating to Tadeo's other operations. Such books, records and accounts will reflect such information as would normally be examined by an independent accountant in performing an audit pursuant to United States generally accepted auditing standards for the purpose of certifying financial statements, and to permit verification thereof by governmental agencies.
perturbation.changed_text: Diplomat may, at Tadeo's discretion, examine those books and records. If Tadeo permits the examination, it will be done as provided in this Section 4. Tadeo is not obligated to allow Diplomat to copy any of its books and records. Such books, records and accounts will be maintained in a manner determined solely by Tadeo.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants Diplomat the right to examine and copy Tadeo's books and records at its own expense. The modified text makes the examination discretionary for Tadeo and removes Diplomat's right to copy the records. It also states that how books and records should be maintained is determined by Tadeo. This directly contradicts the original provision by giving Tadeo full control over a process that was previously defined as a right for Diplomat.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECORDS AND ACCOUNTS. Tadeo will maintain accurate books, records and accounts of all transactions relating to the Services performed by it pursuant to this Agreement. Diplomat may, at Tadeo's discretion, examine those books and records. If Tadeo permits the examination, it will be done as provided in this Section 4. Tadeo is not obligated to allow Diplomat to copy any of its books and records. Such books, records and accounts will be maintained in a manner determined solely by Tadeo. Such books, records and accounts will reflect such information as would normally be examined by an independent accountant in performing an audit pursuant to United States generally accepted auditing standards for the purpose of certifying financial statements, and to permit verification thereof by governmental agencies. Diplomat may make examinations pursuant hereto during Tadeo's usual business hours, and at the place in the continental United States where Tadeo regularly keeps these books and records. Diplomat will be required to notify Tadeo at least five business days before the date of planned examination. If Diplomat's examination is not completed within one month from commencement, Tadeo at any time may require Diplomat to terminate such examination on - 5 - seven days' notice to Diplomat; PROVIDED that Tadeo has cooperated with Diplomat in the examination of such books and records.
perturbation.justification: The changed text gives Tadeo discretion over Diplomat's ability to examine books and records, directly contradicting the implied right granted in the original text regarding Diplomat's access to and control over the examination process. The changed text also states that Tadeo determines how books and records are maintained, conflicting with the requirement that the books and records reflect information as would normally be examined by an independent accountant, therefore the change introduces a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.
